summary: The reproduction confirms that one partial /RPC2 request can monopolize the single-threaded XML-RPC listener and make a concurrent normal call time out until the attacker socket closes.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make a concurrent XML-RPC init call time out while a partial /RPC2 request is held open, then succeed immediately after the blocker closes.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh printed POC_SUCCESS after the helper opened one partial /RPC2 request, observed a concurrent init call time out, released the blocking socket, and then observed the same init call succeed.
verification_method: The exploit helper starts the XML-RPC service, opens one incomplete /RPC2 request, issues a second normal XML-RPC init call with a short timeout, and fails closed unless the second call times out before the blocker is closed and succeeds afterward.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed the exact POC_SUCCESS line confirming timeout-while-blocked and success-after-release behavior.
The helper only reports success when both halves of the oracle hold: the concurrent call must stall first and recover after the blocking socket closes.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the bundled target-repo git bundle on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable snapshot commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
